message:
fix(term): eliminate 3 unwrap() panic sites in iTerm and Kitty image handling

Replace bare .unwrap() calls with safe fallbacks in terminal image
processing paths that handle external/untrusted image data:

iterm.rs:
- candidates.pop().unwrap() → candidates.pop().unwrap_or((width, height))
  in image dimension selection. If the candidate list is empty after
  filtering (e.g., all candidate sizes exceed terminal dimensions), fall
  back to the original image dimensions instead of panicking. The empty
  case is reachable when the image is very small or the terminal very
  large.

kitty.rs (2 sites):
- RgbaImage::from_vec(*width, *height, data.clone()).unwrap() →
  .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("kitty image data size mismatch: {}x{} vs {} bytes"))
  in both the base frame creation path and the frame-by-index compositing
  path. RgbaImage::from_vec returns None when the data buffer length
  doesn't match width*height*4 — this can happen with malformed Kitty
  image protocol data from untrusted applications. Both sites now return
  a descriptive error with dimensions and actual byte count instead of
  panicking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
